message:
{'description': 'List of details for this unassigned order.
', 'type': 'array', 'items': {'type': 'array', 'items': [{'description': 'Reason code.

| Code | Reason |
|------|--------|
| 0    | insufficient vehicles |
| 1    | order service duration exceeds vehicle max_total_time |
| 2    | invalid sequence in ondemand current routes |
| 3    | order depart_after/return_before violation |
| 4    | map snap or geocoding failure |
| 5    | paired/same cluster label orders have map snap or geocoding failure |
| 101  | vehicle max_total_time violation |
| 102  | vehicle max_travel_time violation |
| 103  | vehicle max_distance violation |
| 104  | vehicle max_late_time violation |
| 105  | vehicle max_wait_time violation |
| 200  | vehicle capacity violation |
| 201  | vehicle max_orders_per_route violation |
| 202  | num orders with same cluster_label exceeds vehicle max_orders_per_route |
| 203  | vehicle max_waypoints_per_route violation |
| 204  | renewal violation |
| 205  | break violation |
| 300  | order and vehicle capabilities mismatch |
| 301  | disallowed vehicle or polygon violation |
| 302  | paired/same cluster label orders have capabilities, disallowed vehicle, or polygons violation |
| 400  | start depot(s) has map snap or geocoding failure |
| 401  | end depot(s) has map snap or geocoding failure |
', 'type': 'integer'}, {'description': 'Vehicle ID, `null` if apply to all vehicles.', 'type': 'string'}, {'description': 'Violation value, `null` if not applicable.', 'type': 'string'}]}} is not valid under any of the given schemas

data.current_description: Return the optimization result in json format.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the optimization result from a replanning request. Requires a request ID parameter to identify the specific replanning job. Returns the optimized route(s) with vehicle assignments, stop sequences, and optimization metrics in JSON format. Use this endpoint to poll for results after submitting a replanning request.

message: Operation 'RoutingApIs_calculateRoute' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Routing' is too generic. Description lacks detail about what the operation returns, what parameters are expected, and domain context about routing profiles. Enhanced version explains the input structure (RoutingRequest), output format (geometry, distance, duration, directions), and mentions routing profiles consistent with API context.

data.current_description: Calculate route for a sequence of waypoints.
data.description_suggestion: Calculates an optimized route connecting a sequence of waypoints in the specified order. Accepts a RoutingRequest containing waypoint coordinates, routing profile (e.g., car, bike, pedestrian), and optional constraints. Returns the route geometry, total distance, estimated duration, and turn-by-turn directions. Supports various map providers for routing calculations.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Routing' is too generic. Description lacks detail about what the operation returns, what parameters are expected, and domain context about routing profiles. Enhanced version explains the input structure (RoutingRequest), output format (geometry, distance, duration, directions), and mentions routing profiles consistent with API context.

message: Operation 'RoutingApIs_calculateMatrix' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is appropriate for the domain. Description explains the sync/async behavior but lacks clarity about what the output contains, how to retrieve async results, and units of measurement. Enhanced version clarifies the return value structure, references the GET endpoint for async polling, and explains the units parameter context.

data.current_description: Calculate a matrix of travel distances and durations between a list of locations. Small requests having less than 40,000 elements will be executed synchronously, and http return code will be 200 if succeeded. Larger requests will be executed asynchronously, and http return code will be 202.
data.description_suggestion: Calculates a matrix of travel distances and durations between all pairs of locations in a MatrixRoutingRequest. Supports both synchronous and asynchronous execution: requests with fewer than 40,000 elements execute synchronously and return HTTP 200 with results immediately; larger requests execute asynchronously and return HTTP 202 with a request ID for polling. Returns distance and duration values in the specified units (meters/kilometers, seconds/minutes). Use the GET /matrix/v1 endpoint with the request ID to retrieve asynchronous results.
data.reasoning: Summary is appropriate for the domain. Description explains the sync/async behavior but lacks clarity about what the output contains, how to retrieve async results, and units of measurement. Enhanced version clarifies the return value structure, references the GET endpoint for async polling, and explains the units parameter context.

message: Operation 'matrix_result' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is generic and description is unclear about the polling workflow and required parameters. Enhanced version explains this is for retrieving async results, clarifies the request ID parameter requirement, specifies the output units, and explains the relationship to the POST endpoint for better AI agent workflow understanding.

data.current_description: Return the Matrix Routing API result in units of meters and seconds. For more details regarding the format and error codes, please refer to the 200 response of the POST method.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the results of a matrix routing calculation for an asynchronous request. Requires the request ID parameter from the initial POST /matrix/v1 call. Returns a matrix of travel distances (in meters) and durations (in seconds) between all location pairs. Use this endpoint to poll for results after receiving HTTP 202 from the POST request. Refer to the POST method's 200 response schema for detailed output format and error code documentation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic and description is unclear about the polling workflow and required parameters. Enhanced version explains this is for retrieving async results, clarifies the request ID parameter requirement, specifies the output units, and explains the relationship to the POST endpoint for better AI agent workflow understanding.

message: Operation 'RoutingApIs_calculateRoute' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. data.current_description: Calculate route from a list of GPS tracepoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Match GPS trace points to road network
data.description_suggestion: Performs map matching to align a sequence of GPS trace points to the underlying road network. Accepts a MapMatchingRequest containing GPS coordinates with timestamps and optional accuracy information. Returns the matched route geometry snapped to actual roads, matched waypoint sequences, and confidence scores for each match. Useful for cleaning noisy GPS data from mobile devices or tracking data. Supports various map providers for road network matching.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Map Matching' lacks action clarity. Description is too brief and doesn't explain what map matching does, what the output contains, or use cases. Enhanced version clarifies the purpose (aligning GPS points to roads), explains input structure (GPS coordinates with timestamps), output format (matched geometry, sequences, confidence scores), and provides domain context about use cases and map provider support.

data.current_description: Return the optimization result in json format.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the optimization result for a previously submitted vehicle routing problem. Requires the job_id returned from the initial optimization request. Returns the complete optimized route plan in JSON format, including vehicle assignments, route sequences, and optimization metrics. Use this operation after receiving a job_id from the /vrp/v3 POST endpoint.

data.current_description: Re-optimize an exiting route plan. Returns a `job_id` where you can check the job status and retrieve the result.
data.description_suggestion: Re-optimizes an existing route plan to accommodate changes such as new orders, cancellations, or updated constraints. Requires the current route plan and any modifications in the ReplanRequest. Returns a `job_id` where you can check the job status and retrieve the updated optimized route plan. Useful for dynamic routing scenarios where routes need adjustment after initial optimization.
